revision.text:
'''宗慶大塚古墳'''（そうけおおつかこふん）とは、[[岐阜県]][[本巣市]]宗慶（旧[[本巣郡]][[真正町]]）にある[[前方後円墳]]。岐阜県指定史跡に指定されている。
宗慶古墳、大塚古墳ともいう。
== 概要 == *[[古墳時代]]前期（[[4世紀]]後半～[[5世紀]]前半）の古墳であり、被葬者は不明である。一説によれば、[[美濃国|美濃]][[国造]]（[[三野前国造]]・[[本巣国造]]）[[美濃国]]神大根王（[[彦坐王]]の子。[[開化天皇]]の孫）の墓という。 *前方部はかなりの箇所が崩壊しており、原形をとどめていない。また、後円部も土が流れ落ちて低くなっている。 *周囲は大塚古墳公園として整備されている。周濠跡は散策道として復元されている。
*墳形：[[前方後円墳]] *規模：（現在の状況） **全長 82m **後円部径 42.3m **後円部高さ 4.2m **前方部長 31m
== 所在地 == *岐阜県本巣市宗慶河原田390
== 交通アクセス == === 鉄道 === ;[[樽見鉄道]] :[[北方真桑駅]]より約4km ;[[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]] *大野真正北方線「JA真正支店前」バス停下車、徒歩8分 :[[JR岐阜駅バスターミナル]]（[[岐阜駅]]北）8番のりば、または[[名鉄岐阜のりば]]（[[名鉄岐阜駅]]西）6番のりばより「[[リバーサイドモール]]」「[[大野バスセンター]]」行き。 ;[[もとバス]] *「真正分庁舎」バス停下車、徒歩15分
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:そうけおおつかこふん}} [[Category:岐阜県の古墳]] [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:岐阜県の歴史]] [[Category:都道府県指定史跡]] [[Category:本巣市]]

*墳形：[[前方後円墳]] *規模：（現在の状況） **全長 82m **後円部径 42.3m **後円部高さ 4.2m **前方部長 31m
== 所在地 == *岐阜県本巣市宗慶河原田390
== 交通アクセス == === 鉄道 === ;[[樽見鉄道]] :[[北方真桑駅]]より約4km ;[[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]] *大野真正北方線「JA真正支店前」バス停下車、徒歩8分 :[[JR岐阜駅バスターミナル]]（[[岐阜駅]]北）8番のりば、または[[名鉄岐阜のりば]]（[[名鉄岐阜駅]]西）6番のりばより「[[リバーサイドモール]]」「[[大野バスセンター]]」行き。 ;[[もとバス]] *「真正分庁舎」バス停下車、徒歩15分

*墳形：[[前方後円墳]] *規模：（現在の状況） **全長 82m **後円部径 42.3m **後円部高さ 4.2m **前方部長 31m
== 所在地 == *岐阜県本巣市宗慶河原田390
== 交通アクセス == === 鉄道 === ;[[樽見鉄道]] :[[北方真桑駅]]より約4km ;[[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]] *大野真正北方線「JA真正支店前」バス停下車、徒歩8分 :[[JR岐阜駅バスターミナル]]（[[岐阜駅]]北）8番のりば、または[[名鉄岐阜のりば]]（[[名鉄岐阜駅]]西）6番のりばより「[[リバーサイドモール]]」「[[大野バスセンター]]」行き。 ;[[もとバス]] *「真正分庁舎」バス停下車、徒歩15分
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]
#redirect[[en:Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:Images]]
#redirect[[en:Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{Unreferenced|date=29 July 2015 (Wednesday) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun''' (宗慶大塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Motosu|ja|本巣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Tumulus Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳}} * **Dimensions** (current state): * Total Length: 82 m * Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m * Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m * Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧}}
[[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:History of Motosu, Gifu]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]

{{Unreferenced|date=29 July 2015 (Wednesday) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun''' (宗慶大塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Motosu|ja|本巣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Tumulus Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳}} * **Dimensions** (current state): * Total Length: 82 m * Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m * Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m * Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧}}

== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.

{{Unreferenced|date=29 July 2015 (Wednesday) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun''' (宗慶大塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Motosu|ja|本巣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Tumulus Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳}} * **Dimensions** (current state): * Total Length: 82 m * Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m * Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m * Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.

{{Unreferenced|date=29 July 2015 (Wednesday) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun''' (宗慶大塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Motosu|ja|本巣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Tumulus Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳}} * **Dimensions** (current state): * Total Length: 82 m * Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m * Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m * Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧}}

== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Tumulus Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳}} * **Dimensions** (current state): * Total Length: 82 m * Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m * Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m * Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.

{{Unreferenced|date=29 July 2015 (Wednesday) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun''' (宗慶大塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Motosu|ja|本巣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Tumulus Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳}} * **Dimensions** (current state): * Total Length: 82 m * Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m * Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m * Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧}}

It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. * **Tumulus Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * **Dimensions** (current state): * Total Length: 82 m * Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m * Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m * Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=29 July 2015 (Wednesday) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun''' (宗慶大塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Motosu|ja|本巣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Tumulus Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * **Dimensions** (current state): * Total Length: 82 m * Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m * Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m * Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Unreferenced|date=29 July 2015 (Wednesday) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun''' (宗慶大塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Motosu|ja|本巣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Tumulus Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * **Dimensions** (current state): * Total Length: 82 m * Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m * Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m * Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧}}

== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Tumulus Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * **Dimensions** (current state): * Total Length: 82 m * Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m * Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m * Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧}}

It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * **Dimensions** (current state): * Total Length: 82 m * Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m * Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m * Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧}}

revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=29 July 2015 (Wednesday) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun''' (宗慶大塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Motosu|ja|本巣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * **Dimensions** (current state): ** Total Length: 82 m ** Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m ** Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m ** Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧}}

One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * **Dimensions** (current state): ** Total Length: 82 m ** Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m ** Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m ** Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧}}

{{Unreferenced|date=29 July 2015 (Wednesday) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun''' (宗慶大塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Motosu|ja|本巣市}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * **Dimensions** (current state): ** Total Length: 82 m ** Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m ** Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m ** Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧}}

=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス|12=draft}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅|12=draft}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅|12=draft}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター|12=draft}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス|12=draft}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Return the see also]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=29 July 2015 (Wednesday) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun''' (宗慶大塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Motosu|ja|本巣市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|12=draft}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡|12=draft}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王|12=draft}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|12=draft}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} * **Dimensions** (current state): ** Total Length: 82 m ** Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m ** Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m ** Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=draft}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅|12=draft}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス|12=draft}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅|12=draft}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅|12=draft}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣|12=draft}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター|12=draft}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス|12=draft}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}}
<!--Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun-->

14 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:宗慶大塚古墳 up to rev 97999335
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2015年7月29日 (水) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|宗慶大塚古墳]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''宗慶大塚古墳'''（そうけおおつかこふん）とは、[[岐阜県]][[本巣市]]宗慶にある[[前方後円墳]]。岐阜県指定史跡に指定されている<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=宗慶大塚古墳 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>。
宗慶古墳、大塚古墳ともいう。
== 概要 == *[[古墳時代]]前期（[[4世紀]]後半～[[5世紀]]前半）の古墳であり、被葬者は不明である。一説によれば、[[美濃国|美濃]][[国造]]（[[三野前国造]]・[[本巣国造]]）神大根王（[[彦坐王]]の子。[[開化天皇]]の孫）の墓という。 *前方部はかなりの箇所が崩壊しており、原形をとどめていない。また、後円部も土が流れ落ちて低くなっている。 *周囲は大塚古墳公園として整備されている<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=本巣市 宗慶大塚古墳公園 4月11日開園|publisher=テイコク|accessdate=2015-07-29|language=日本語}}</ref>。周濠跡は散策道として復元されている。
*墳形：[[前方後円墳]] *規模：（現在の状況） **全長 82m **後円部径 42.3m **後円部高さ 4.2m **前方部長 31m
== 所在地 == *岐阜県本巣市宗慶河原田390
== 交通アクセス == === 鉄道 === ;[[樽見鉄道]] :[[北方真桑駅]]より約4km === バス === ;[[岐阜乗合自動車|岐阜バス]] *大野真正北方線「JA真正支店前」バス停下車、徒歩8分 :[[JR岐阜駅バスターミナル]]（[[岐阜駅]]北）8番のりば、または[[名鉄岐阜のりば]]（[[名鉄岐阜駅]]西）6番のりばより「[[イオンタウン本巣]]」「[[大野バスセンター]]」行き。 ;[[本巣市市営バス]] *弾正線「真正分庁舎」バス停下車、徒歩15分
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
{{デフォルトソート:そうけおおつかこふん}} [[Category:岐阜県の古墳]] [[Category:岐阜県指定史跡]] [[Category:本巣市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * **Dimensions** (current state): ** Total Length: 82 m ** Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m ** Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m ** Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.

It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * **Dimensions** (current state): ** Total Length: 82 m ** Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m ** Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m ** Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.

[[sn:Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%AE%97%E6%85%B6%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[en:Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]]
<!--Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun-->

{{Unreferenced|date=29 July 2015 (Wednesday) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun''' (宗慶大塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Motosu|ja|本巣市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu|qq=}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|ja|岐阜県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture|qq=}}. It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Historic Site|qq=}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}, a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozakari|qq=}} and grandson of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|ja|開化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kaika|qq=}}. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} * **Dimensions** (current state): ** Total Length: 82 m ** Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m ** Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m ** Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}} * Approximately 4 km from {{ill|Kitagata-Makuwa Station|ja|北方真桑駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitagata-Makuwa Station|qq=}}.
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Bus|qq=}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from {{ill|JR Gifu Station|ja|岐阜駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Gifu Station|qq=}} Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or {{ill|Meitetsu Gifu Station|ja|名鉄岐阜駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meitetsu Gifu Station|qq=}} (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aeon Town Motosu|qq=}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Bus Center|qq=}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu City Municipal Bus|qq=}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:岐阜県の古墳
revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=29 July 2015 (Wednesday) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun''' (宗慶大塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Motosu, Gifu|Motosu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Historic Site|qq=}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}, a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozakari|qq=}} and grandson of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} * **Dimensions** (current state): ** Total Length: 82 m ** Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m ** Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m ** Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}} * Approximately 4 km from [[:en:Kitagata-Makuwa Station|Kitagata-Makuwa Station]].
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Bus|qq=}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from [[:en:Gifu Station|JR Gifu Station]] Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]] (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aeon Town Motosu|qq=}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Bus Center|qq=}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu City Municipal Bus|qq=}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|宗慶大塚古墳|version=97999335|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Japan]] [[Category:History of Motosu, Gifu]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]]
[[sn:Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]]}}
[[sn:Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:%E5%AE%97%E6%85%B6%E5%A4%A7%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[ja:&action=edit]] [[en:Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]]

One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}, a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozakari|qq=}} and grandson of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} * **Dimensions** (current state): ** Total Length: 82 m ** Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m ** Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m ** Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}} * Approximately 4 km from [[:en:Kitagata-Makuwa Station|Kitagata-Makuwa Station]].
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Bus|qq=}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from [[:en:Gifu Station|JR Gifu Station]] Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]] (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aeon Town Motosu|qq=}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Bus Center|qq=}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu City Municipal Bus|qq=}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.

== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}} * Approximately 4 km from [[:en:Kitagata-Makuwa Station|Kitagata-Makuwa Station]].

* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} * **Dimensions** (current state): ** Total Length: 82 m ** Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m ** Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m ** Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}} * Approximately 4 km from [[:en:Kitagata-Makuwa Station|Kitagata-Makuwa Station]].
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜乗合自動車}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from [[:en:Gifu Station|JR Gifu Station]] Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]] (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aeon Town Motosu|qq=}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Bus Center|qq=}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu City Municipal Bus|qq=}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=29 July 2015 (Wednesday) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun''' (宗慶大塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Motosu, Gifu|Motosu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}, a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozakari|qq=}} and grandson of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} * **Dimensions** (current state): ** Total Length: 82 m ** Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m ** Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m ** Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}} * Approximately 4 km from [[:en:Kitagata-Makuwa Station|Kitagata-Makuwa Station]].
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜乗合自動車}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from [[:en:Gifu Station|JR Gifu Station]] Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]] (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aeon Town Motosu|qq=}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Bus Center|qq=}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu City Municipal Bus|qq=}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.

The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} * **Dimensions** (current state): ** Total Length: 82 m ** Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m ** Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m ** Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}} * Approximately 4 km from [[:en:Kitagata-Makuwa Station|Kitagata-Makuwa Station]].
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜乗合自動車}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from [[:en:Gifu Station|JR Gifu Station]] Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]] (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aeon Town Motosu|qq=}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Bus Center|qq=}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu City Municipal Bus|qq=}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}}

== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}, a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozakari|qq=}} and grandson of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} * **Dimensions** (current state): ** Total Length: 82 m ** Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m ** Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m ** Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=}} * Approximately 4 km from [[:en:Kitagata-Makuwa Station|Kitagata-Makuwa Station]].
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜乗合自動車}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from [[:en:Gifu Station|JR Gifu Station]] Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]] (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aeon Town Motosu|qq=}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Bus Center|qq=}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu City Municipal Bus|qq=}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.

{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.430194|N|136.667083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11451942]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=29 July 2015 (Wednesday) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun''' (宗慶大塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Motosu, Gifu|Motosu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}, a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozakari|qq=}} and grandson of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} * **Dimensions** (current state): ** Total Length: 82 m ** Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m ** Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m ** Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|qq=|1=Tarumi Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543718}} * Approximately 4 km from [[:en:Kitagata-Makuwa Station|Kitagata-Makuwa Station]].
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Bus|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜乗合自動車|1=Gifu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5560101}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from [[:en:Gifu Station|JR Gifu Station]] Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]] (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aeon Town Motosu|qq=|1=AEON TOWN Motosu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q54555201}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Bus Center|qq=|1=Ōno Bus Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439494}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu City Municipal Bus|qq=|1=Motosu City Municipal Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280046}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|en|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.430194|N|136.667083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11451942]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:28, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=29 July 2015 (Wednesday) 09:42 (UTC)}} [[File:Soukei otsuka kofun.jpg|thumb|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|25|48.7|N|136|40|1.5|E|region:JP-21|display=title}} '''Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun''' (宗慶大塚古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Motosu, Gifu|Motosu]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|岐阜県指定史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Gifu Prefecture.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/shiseki/motosusi/soukei.html |title=Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2012-08-04}}</ref>
It is also referred to as Sōkei Kofun or Ōtsuka Kofun.
== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}, a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozakari|qq=}} and grandson of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.

One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}, a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozakari|qq=}} and grandson of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.

== Overview == * The tomb dates to the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (late 4th century to early 5th century). The identity of the buried individual is unknown. One theory suggests it is the tomb of {{ill|Kami Ōne|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}, a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] (Mino and Motosu Provinces), the son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikozakari|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikozakari|qq=}} and grandson of [[:en:Emperor Kaika|Emperor Kaika]]. * The front section of the tomb has significantly collapsed and does not retain its original shape. The rear circular section has also eroded and lost height. * The surrounding area has been developed as Ōtsuka Kofun Park.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://teikoku-eng.co.jp/jisseki/1504_soukei/soukei.html|title=Motosu City Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun Park Opened April 11 |publisher=Teikoku |accessdate=2015-07-29|language=Japanese}}</ref> The remnants of the surrounding moat have been restored as walking paths.
* **Kofun Type**: {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} * **Dimensions** (current state): ** Total Length: 82 m ** Rear Circular Section Diameter: 42.3 m ** Rear Circular Section Height: 4.2 m ** Front Section Length: 31 m
== Location == * Address: Kawarada 390, Sōkei, Motosu City, Gifu Prefecture, Japan
== Transportation Access == === By Train === ; {{ill|Tarumi Railway|ja|樽見鉄道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarumi Railway|1=Tarumi Railway|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11543718}} * Approximately 4 km from [[:en:Kitagata-Makuwa Station|Kitagata-Makuwa Station]].
=== By Bus === ; {{ill|Gifu Bus|ja|岐阜バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Bus|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜乗合自動車|1=Gifu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5560101}} * Alight at "JA Masano Branch" bus stop on the Ōno-Masano-Kitagata Line, an 8-minute walk. ** Departures from [[:en:Gifu Station|JR Gifu Station]] Bus Terminal (North, Platform 8) or [[:en:Meitetsu Gifu Station|Meitetsu Gifu Station]] (West, Platform 6), bound for {{ill|Aeon Town Motosu|ja|イオンタウン本巣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aeon Town Motosu|1=AEON TOWN Motosu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q54555201}} or {{ill|Ōno Bus Center|ja|大野バスセンター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Bus Center|1=Ōno Bus Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439494}}. ; {{ill|Motosu City Municipal Bus|ja|本巣市市営バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu City Municipal Bus|1=Motosu City Municipal Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11280046}} * Alight at "Masano Branch Office" bus stop on the Danjō Line, a 15-minute walk.
